definition: A problem has an algorithmic procedure that returns the correct result for all valid inputs.
A yes-or-no problem has a procedure that always halts with the correct answer.
A procedure accepts yes cases but may not halt on no cases.
No general decider exists for all valid inputs.
Another term often used for recognizable.
A mathematical model of mechanical computation.
The claim that effectively calculable functions are computable by a Turing machine.
A transformation showing that solving one problem would solve another.
The problem of deciding whether an arbitrary program halts on an input.
